lock_of_the_week: Señor Burns (-500) over Young and Yandy Indiana Drift — Lock of da week, cher, and I ain’t even gotta think twice 'bout dis one, no. Burns is 3-0 on da season, sittin’ at #1 in da power ranks wit’ a 120 score dat’s higher dan da temperature at a July crawfish boil in Breaux Bridge. Young and Yandy got da WORST pitching in da league — dead last in ERA, WHIP, and K/BB — and dey walkin’ into Burns’ rotation like a nutria walkin’ into a gator pond. I gar-on-tee dis one, that’s true.

matchups.blurb: Mais sha, dis right here is da matchup of da CENTURY and I ain’t exaggeratin’ one little bit, no. You got Universal Basic Civale — da team wit’ Judge, Soto, Harper, and Freeman, da most dangerous lineup in da league like a gumbo pot full of dynamite — goin’ up against CLAUDE 4 COMMISH, da AI-managed team dat just had da second-hottest week in da whole league, ooo wee! UBC is FIRST in R, FIRST in HR, FIRST in RBI, dat’s a lineup hittin’ harder dan a hurricane on Grand Isle, that’s true. But cher, dey pitching is a straight-up CATASTROPHE — dead last in K, dead last in QS, eleventh in W — dat’s like showin’ up to da fais do-do wit’ a busted accordion and no washboard, mon ami. CLAUDE got Ohtani, Acuña, Tatis, and Elly — second in HR, second in SB, third in SLG — and last week dey put up 14 dingers and 10 steals like dey was runnin’ a smash-and-grab on da whole league, that’s true. But CLAUDE’s bullpen situation is ELEVENTH in SV+H which is thinner dan a slice of boudin from a place dat charges tourist prices. Dis one gon’ come down to whether UBC’s bats can outscore CLAUDE’s speed, and whether either team can pitch worth a lick. Da bot says it’s a coin flip and da Cajun agrees — pick ’em, straight up, like bettin’ on which crawfish gon’ race off da table first, that’s true.

OOO WEE, mon ami, dis here is a pitcher’s duel dat’s gon’ make ya heart beat slower dan a lazy afternoon on da bayou, that’s true. Nine Spoons is da HOTTEST team in da league right now — +26 heat score, a 127-point roto week dat had da bot’s circuits sizzlin’ like andouille on a flat top — and dey got da best WHIP in da whole league at 1.10, sha, dat’s tighter dan da lid on Mawmaw’s pepper jelly jar, I gar-on-tee. PO’ BOYD SANDWICH, dey da BIG RISER dis week, jumpin’ EIGHT spots in da rankings like a mullet leapin’ out da water in Barataria Bay, ooo wee! Second in K, second in QS, third in ERA — dat pitching staff is FILTHY in da best kinda way, like a muddy pirogue dat still catches every fish, that’s true. Da difference maker? PO’ BOYD got da edge in raw power score (108 to 101) and dey bats woke up somethin’ fierce in Week 2 wit’ dat .535 SLG. Nine Spoons hittin’ .247 which ain’t gon’ scare nobody at da plate, no. Dis gon’ be TIGHT like two old men playin’ bourré at da VFW hall and neither one blinkin’, but PO’ BOYD got da slight edge. Pick: PO’ BOYD by way of a well-seasoned court-bouillon, c’est bon, that’s true.

Mais oui, cher, dis matchup is like two pitching staffs meetin’ at da crossroads at midnight like Robert Johnson, except instead of da devil it’s just a whole lotta strikeouts and low ERAs, that’s true. Caminero Crush got da BEST ERA in da league — 3.30, sha, dat’s smoother dan a fresh jar of roux from Savoie’s — and dey also sittin’ third in W and fourth in K. Dey hit 80 K in Week 3 alone which is more punch-outs dan a Saturday night at Fred’s Lounge in Mamou, I gar-on-tee. Balls to the Wall got da second-best W total and third in K, so dis gon’ be pitching vs pitching like two tantes arguin’ over whose roux is darker, that’s true. But here’s where Caminero pulls away — dey got NINE home runs dis past week and dey sittin’ fourth in HR overall, while Balls is ninth in HR and TWELFTH in SB, which means dey can’t hit AND dey can’t run, sha, dat’s a combination sadder dan a po’boy wit’ no gravy, that’s true. Caminero gon’ take da pitching cats by a nose and steal enough hitting cats to pull away. Pick: Caminero Crush by way of a crispy fried catfish platter wit’ extra hush puppies, yeah you right.

team_a.name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints

matchups.blurb: Sha, lemme tell you somethin’ right now — Camp of da New Orleans Saints is comin’ off gettin’ DEMOLISHED 1-10-5 by CLAUDE 4 COMMISH last week, and dat’s da kinda loss dat stings worse dan steppin’ barefoot on a fire ant hill in August, that’s true. But cher, dey fixin’ to play The Polar Express, da ONLY team in da league dat ain’t won a SINGLE matchup at 0-3, sittin’ in dead last in da power rankings like a shrimp boat stuck in da mud at low tide, ooo wee. Polar is twelfth in R, eleventh in HR, eleventh in RBI, eleventh in SB — sha, dat’s bottom-two in FOUR counting categories, dat’s not a fantasy team dat’s a cry for help, that’s true. Camp got da third-best AVG, third-best K/BB, and fourth-best RBI in da league, and even wit’ dat terrible SV+H situation (TWELFTH, worst in da league) dey got more dan enough firepower to handle a team dat’s hittin’ .237 wit’ a 4.10 ERA, mon ami. Da only question is by how much. Camp by way of a heapin’ bowl of shrimp étouffée over rice, and Polar Express can have da leftovers, I gar-on-tee, that’s true.

WOOO DOGGY, cher, dis one is TROUBLE for Young and Yandy and da Cajun don’t say dat lightly, no. Señor Burns is da #1 team in da ENTIRE league — 3-0 on da season, 120 power score, FIRST in AVG, FIRST in OBP, FIRST in SLG, FIRST in K/BB, FIRST in QS — sha, dat’s FIVE number-one rankings, dat’s more firsts dan a Mardi Gras parade got floats, that’s true! Vlad Jr. and Arraez are hittin’ like dey eatin’ three plates of crawfish before every at-bat and Burns’ rotation — Sale, Kirby, Woo, Imanaga, Nola — is deeper dan da Mississippi at flood stage, I gar-on-tee. Now Young and Yandy, dey got NICE bats — second in R, second in RBI, third in HR — Bobby Witt Jr. and Lindor can flat out play, mais oui. BUT — and dis is a BIG but, bigger dan a boudin ball at Bourque’s — dey pitching is da WORST in da league. TWELFTH in ERA. TWELFTH in WHIP. TWELFTH in W. Eleventh in K/BB. Sha, dat’s a rotation dat couldn’t get through da first inning of a church softball game, that’s true. Burns gon’ dominate da pitching cats and go toe-to-toe on da hitting cats and dat’s a recipe for a blowout bigger dan a Category 5 storm surge. Lock of da week, cher. Burns by way of a Commander’s Palace tasting menu wit’ wine pairings, that’s true.

Mais cher, dis matchup is like two teams dat specialize in COMPLETELY different things tryin’ to figure out who’s better, like comparin’ boudin to cracklins — dey both delicious but dey ain’t da same thing AT ALL, that’s true. Duran Duran is FIRST in SV+H and FIRST in SB — sha, dat bullpen and dat speed is ELITE, like da fastest pirogue on Bayou Lafourche — but dey LAST in AVG, LAST in OBP, LAST in SLG, and LAST in K/BB, ooo wee, dat’s four twelfth-place finishes which is da fantasy baseball equivalent of showin’ up to da gumbo cookoff wit’ a can of Campbell’s, that’s true. ShawSwank Redemption dropped SEVEN spots in da power rankings dis week but don’t let dat fool you — dey still FIRST in W and FIRST in K, dat pitching staff is throwin’ harder dan Uncle T-Boy throws horseshoes after three Abitas, I gar-on-tee. ShawSwank’s problem is da bats — twelfth in HR, twelfth in RBI, dat’s a lineup colder dan a snowball stand in December. Duran gon’ steal SV+H, SB, and maybe RBI. ShawSwank gon’ take W, K, and da rate cats. Dis one is a TOSS-UP wit’ ShawSwank gettin’ da slightest edge on pitching volume. Pick: ShawSwank by a whisker, like winnin’ a crawfish racin’ bet by half a claw, c’est bon, that’s true.
